channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700 km from Border
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, some 700 km from the Ukrainian border, President Zelenskiy said Friday, in the latest of Kyiv’s strikes on Russian oil facilities.

item.title: 🇪🇦 UAE Presidential Advisor: 50-50 Chance of US-Iran Deal
item.description: UAE presidential advisor Anwar Gargash said there is a "50-50 chance" of a US-Iran peace agreement, but stressed any settlement must address root causes of regional instability to avoid future conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Red Cross Volunteers Combat Ebola Misinformation in Congo
item.description: Volunteers are going door-to-door to combat misinformation about Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where the WHO has raised the outbreak risk to "very high" amid rapid spread.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Freed Gaza Flotilla Activists Allege Israeli Abuse
item.description: Activists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a flotilla trying to bring aid to Gaza alleged they were subjected to abuse including sexual violence, organizers said Friday.

item.title: 🇪🇪 Estonia Spy Chief: Putin Running Out of Time in Ukraine
item.description: Estonia’s foreign intelligence chief told CNN that Russian President Vladimir Putin is running out of time to win his war against Ukraine, facing battlefield stalemate and growing troubles at home.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU warns of stagflation and fiscal crisis risks from Iran war energy shock
item.description: European officials say the surge in energy prices caused by the Iran war has created a stagflationary trend, warning governments against generous fiscal support that could trigger a sovereign debt crisis or higher ECB rates.

item.title: ☢️ Russia sent more nuclear warheads to Belarus in risky escalation
item.description: Russia deployed additional nuclear warheads to Belarus for the first time during joint drills, with analysts calling it a risky move for Minsk amid the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇭🇳 Gang violence kills at least 25 in Honduras
item.description: Gunmen carried out two separate attacks in Honduras as the government continues a crackdown on organized crime, killing at least 25 people including 6 police officers.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta to hold referendum on whether to remain in Canada
item.description: Voters in the western province will decide next October whether they want to stay in Canada or move ahead with a binding independence referendum process.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Falters in Ukraine as Economy Weakens and Casualties Mount
item.description: Independent estimates suggest Russia economy is faltering under war costs, while Kyiv claims 83,000 Russian casualties so far in 2026. Top Ukrainian officials say Moscows woes are growing.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Opens $9 Billion Island Megaport Near Strait of Malacca
item.description: The Great Nicobar project will allow India to monitor activities near the Strait of Malacca, sharpening Chinas Malacca dilemma as regional maritime competition intensifies.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Court Removes Opposition Leader in Major Blow to Democracy
item.description: A Turkish court issued a ruling that effectively removed the head of the main opposition CHP party, deepening Turkeys political crisis as opposition vows resistance.

item.title: 🇳🇱🇮🇱 Netherlands Bans Imports from Israeli Settlements
item.description: The Dutch government agreed to ban imports of goods produced in Jewish settlements in Israeli-occupied territories, marking a significant European policy shift.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Core Inflation Hits 4-Year Low Before Energy Shock
item.description: Japans annual core inflation slowed to a four-year low in April due to fuel subsidies, though analysts expect surging costs from the Middle East war to accelerate price growth in coming months.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇫🇷 Israel Deports 37 French Gaza Flotilla Activists to Turkey
item.description: Israel is deporting 37 French nationals who were part of a Gaza-bound aid flotilla to Turkey, according to Frances Foreign Ministry. The activists were among a group intercepted while attempting to breach the Gaza blockade.

item.title: 🇨🇳💰 China Denies Forcing Tech Firms to Reject Foreign Investment
item.description: Chinas powerful state planner NDRC said the government has never required Chinese technology companies to reject foreign investment, responding to questions about whether Beijing plans to ask firms to refuse US capital amid ongoing technology tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇷⚖️ Greek Terror Group Leader Released at 82 After Decades in Prison
item.description: The head of Greeces far-left November 17 militant group, which killed four American officials and 19 others, has been released from a maximum-security prison at age 82. The group was active from 1975 to 2002, targeting US military personnel, diplomats, and Greek figures.

item.title: 🔒⚖️ Texas Sues Meta and WhatsApp Over Encryption Privacy Claims
item.description: The Texas Attorney Generals Office sued WhatsApp and Meta Platforms alleging they misled consumers about the strength and scope of WhatsApps encryption measures. Meta denied the allegations, calling them baseless.

item.title: 🦠🌍 WHO Africa Head Warns Ebola Outbreak Risk Should Not Be Underestimated
item.description: The WHO regional director for Africa cautioned that just one case could spread the Ebola virus beyond the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, urging heightened vigilance as no vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Considered Leaving OPEC for Three Years Over Oil Strategy
item.description: A senior UAE presidential adviser revealed the country contemplated leaving OPEC for three years, believing the world is nearing the "autumn of the hydrocarbon age" and needs to maximize oil revenues while possible.

item.title: 🇺🇳🇺🇦 UN Refugee Agency Loses Million in Aid After Russian Strike on Ukraine Warehouse
item.description: A Russian missile struck a UN refugee agency warehouse in Dnipro, destroying $1 million worth of humanitarian aid destined for civilians in eastern Ukraine.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 US Pauses $14 Billion Taiwan Arms Sale Citing Iran War Stockpile Needs
item.description: Taiwan's presidential office said it has not been notified of any changes to military sales, after a US official indicated a pause on a major arms package due to weapons stockpile demands from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇻🇪🇮🇳🛢️ Venezuelan Oil Emerges as India's Third-Largest Supplier Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: As the Iran war closes the Strait of Hormuz, Venezuela has become India's third-largest crude oil supplier this month, highlighting the global energy supply chain shifts caused by the conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇦🛢️ Saudi Oil Exports Surge 37% as Kingdom Bypasses Hormuz via Red Sea Route
item.description: Saudi Arabia reported a 37.4% surge in oil exports in March, the highest level in 3.5 years, as the East-West pipeline allowed crude shipments to bypass the Strait of Hormuz disruptions and reach global markets through the Red Sea port of Yanbu.

item.title: 🇦🇫⚖️ Taliban Decree Formalizes Child Marriage Rules, UN Expresses Grave Concern
item.description: The UN warned that a new Taliban decree on marriage separation implies child marriage is permitted, saying girls' silence at puberty can be interpreted as consent. The Afghan justice ministry defended the decree as following Islamic law.
